Variables.details: Declares a reassignable, block-scoped local variable, optionally initializing it to a value.
Declares a block-scoped local variable whose value cannot be reassigned after initialization.
Declares a function-scoped or globally-scoped variable, optionally initializing it to a value. Generally avoided in modern JS due to hoisting issues.

Functions.details: Declares a function with specified parameters and a body. Declarations are hoisted to the top of their scope.
A compact alternative to a traditional function expression. Arrow functions do not have their own 'this' or 'arguments' bindings.
A function that runs as soon as it is defined. Often used to create a private scope.

Arrays.desc: Add item to end of array
Arrays.details: Appends one or more elements to the end of an array and returns the new length of the array.
Removes the last element from an array and returns that element. This method changes the length of the array.
Removes the first element from an array and returns that removed element. This method changes the length of the array.

Arrays.desc: Add item to beginning of array
Arrays.details: Adds one or more elements to the beginning of an array and returns the new length of the array.

Arrays.desc: Create a new array with results of calling a function
Arrays.details: Creates a new array populated with the results of calling a provided function on every element in the calling array.

Arrays.desc: Create a new array with elements that pass the test
Arrays.details: Creates a shallow copy of a portion of a given array, filtered down to just the elements from the given array that pass the test implemented by the provided function.

Arrays.desc: Reduce the array to a single value
Arrays.details: Executes a user-supplied 'reducer' callback function on each element of the array, in order, passing in the return value from the calculation on the preceding element.
Arrays.example: const sum = numbers.reduce((acc, curr) => acc + curr, 0);

Arrays.desc: Return the first element that satisfies the test
Arrays.details: Returns the first element in the provided array that satisfies the provided testing function. If no values satisfy the testing function, undefined is returned.

Arrays.desc: Determine whether an array includes a certain value
Arrays.details: Determines whether an array includes a certain value among its entries, returning true or false as appropriate.

Objects.desc: Return an array of a given object's property names
Objects.details: Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable string-keyed property names.

Objects.desc: Return an array of a given object's property values
Objects.details: Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable string-keyed property values.

Objects.desc: Return an array of a given object's [key, value] pairs
Objects.details: Returns an array of a given object's own enumerable string-keyed property [key, value] pairs.

Objects.desc: Copy values of all enumerable properties from source to target
Objects.details: Copies all enumerable own properties from one or more source objects to a target object. It returns the modified target object.
Allows an iterable such as an object expression or array to be expanded in places where zero or more arguments or elements are expected.

Promises & Async.details: Constructs a Promise object which represents the eventual completion (or failure) of an asynchronous operation and its resulting value.
Promises & Async.example: const p = new Promise((resolve, reject) => { setTimeout(resolve, 1000); });

Promises & Async.details: Schedules functions to be called when the promise is either fulfilled or rejected.
Defines a function that returns a Promise and allows the use of the 'await' keyword inside it.

Promises & Async.desc: Wait for a promise to resolve (inside async function)
Promises & Async.details: Pauses the execution of the async function until the Promise is settled (either fulfilled or rejected).

Promises & Async.desc: Wait for all promises to be resolved
Promises & Async.details: Takes an iterable of promises and returns a single Promise that resolves when all of the input promises have resolved.

DOM Manipulation.desc: Return the first element that matches the selector
DOM Manipulation.details: Returns the first Element within the document that matches the specified selector, or group of selectors. If no matches are found, null is returned.

DOM Manipulation.desc: Return a static NodeList of elements matching the selector
DOM Manipulation.details: Returns a static (not live) NodeList representing a list of the document's elements that match the specified group of selectors.

DOM Manipulation.desc: Return the element with the specified ID
DOM Manipulation.details: Returns an Element object representing the element whose id property matches the specified string.
In an HTML document, the document.createElement() method creates the HTML element specified by tagName.

DOM Manipulation.desc: Add a node to the end of the list of children of a specified parent
DOM Manipulation.details: Adds a node to the end of the list of children of a specified parent node.

DOM Manipulation.desc: Attach an event handler to the specified element
DOM Manipulation.details: Sets up a function that will be called whenever the specified event is delivered to the target.

JSON.desc: Parse a JSON string, constructing the JS value or object
JSON.details: Parses a JSON string, constructing the JavaScript value or object described by the string.

JSON.desc: Convert a JS object or value to a JSON string
JSON.details: Converts a JavaScript object or value to a JSON string, optionally replacing values if a replacer function is specified.
